title: Hypocrisy (Nifaq)
subtitle: The Lowest Pit of Hellfire

verses.arabic: إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الْأَسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ وَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُمْ نَصِيرًا
verses.translation: Indeed, the hypocrites will be in the lowest depths of the Fire — and never will you find for them a helper.

verses.arabic: إِذَا جَاءَكَ الْمُنَافِقُونَ قَالُوا نَشْهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُهُ وَاللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ لَكَاذِبُونَ
verses.translation: When the hypocrites come to you, [O Muhammad], they say, 'We testify that you are the Messenger of Allah.' And Allah knows that you are His Messenger, and Allah testifies that the hypocrites are liars.

verses.arabic: الْمُنَافِقُونَ وَالْمُنَافِقَاتُ بَعْضُهُم مِّن بَعْضٍ ۚ يَأْمُرُونَ بِالْمُنكَرِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَقْبِضُونَ أَيْدِيَهُمْ ۚ نَسُوا اللَّهَ فَنَسِيَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
verses.translation: The hypocrite men and hypocrite women are of one another. They enjoin what is wrong and forbid what is right and close their hands. They have forgotten Allah, so He has forgotten them. Indeed, the hypocrites — it is they who are the defiantly disobedient.

brief_explanation: Nifaq is the most dangerous form of deviation — outward Islam concealing inner disbelief or enmity toward the deen. Allah reserves the lowest level of Hellfire (al-Darak al-Asfal) specifically for the munafiqeen, a punishment worse than for open disbelievers. The hypocrite lies to Allah and the believers, commands evil and forbids good, and abandons Allah until Allah abandons them. The Quran dedicates entire passages (Surah Al-Munafiqun, parts of Al-Baqarah and At-Tawbah) to exposing their traits: they use oaths as shields, they defect in battle, and their hearts are sealed. Anyone exhibiting these traits — dishonesty in covenant, treachery in trust, vulgarity in disputes — carries signs of nifaq as warned by the Prophet.
capital_punishment: The munafiq who outwardly professes Islam cannot be executed on the basis of nifaq alone, since Allah has concealed their inner state as a mercy — to prevent killing those who merely appear hypocritical. The Prophet (peace be upon him) was informed of specific munafiqeen yet did not execute them, saying 'so that people do not say Muhammad kills his companions.' If a person openly apostatizes or wages war against Islam, the rulings of apostasy or hiraba apply.
